libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Keep or add test JVM arguments when covering Lucene internals that access java.nio direct buffers or jdk.internal.ref: --add-opens=java.base/java.nio=ALL-UNNAMED and --add-opens=java.base/jdk.internal.ref=ALL-UNNAMED. The javac failure itself is an API-change issue: custom AttributeImpl test subclasses for Lucene 6.0.0 must implement reflectWith(AttributeReflector), because it became abstract.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #5851 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.apache.lucene:lucene-core:6.0.0; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: Adding setup will not resolve the reported compilation error unless the test source is updated for the Lucene 6 AttributeImpl API.

Avoid adding analyzer/queryparser dependencies just to create higher-level search examples; lucene-core is intentionally standalone and can be covered through its own store, util, codec, attribute, and index primitives.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No extra Maven dependency or Docker service is needed; lucene-core 6.0.0 has no runtime dependencies beyond java.base, but meaningful tests that exercise MMapDirectory/RamUsageEstimator may need JVM module opens already used by the existing scaffold.
